# Scorched-Earth Erdős Sweep — Turn 103

This is turn 103. Turns 1 through 102 are consolidated in `turns_history__frontiermath_q2_galois.md` (attached). Re-read it before answering.
Also re-read `mega_prompt.md` and `problem_statement.md`.

CRITICAL — use ONLY the files attached to THIS message. Do NOT open, import, retrieve, or cite any file from your File Library / previously uploaded files, even one named similarly (e.g. another `turns_history…md`). Other unrelated problems use similarly named history files in the same account, and importing one will silently switch you onto the wrong problem. If the attached `turns_history__frontiermath_q2_galois.md` appears missing, do NOT continue from partial context. Reply only with `MISSING_REQUIRED_ATTACHMENT` and name the missing file(s); never substitute a library file.

If this turn drifts into hammering one specific case or sub-instance, step back and ask whether the case is revealing a structural pattern that would close many cases at once. Prefer arguments whose conclusion is a uniform statement.

End this response with ESTABLISHED / GAP / NEXT + tag.
Continue with Turn 103.

Note that a source being available does not imply that the user has synced data from that source yet; in case they haven't synced / connected a source yet, no documents will be returned from that source. If you get no results after a filtered search, you can also try adjusting the source_filter or leaving it empty to query more sources.

## Turn 103 — one exact-image induction now contains Turns 74–102

The candidate remains unchanged:

```text
sigma,tau,x0,x1

sigma2 = sigma^40491355905
u0 = (x0*tau)^40491355905
u1 = (x1*tau)^40491355905
d0 = u0*x0^-1
z0 = x0^sigma2
c0 = d0^-1*z0^-1*d0*z0

x0^2*x1^4*u1^-1*(x1^sigma)*c0
```

The tame relation \(\tau^\sigma=\tau^2\) is implicit. In the mathematical presentation, each displayed exponent \(40491355905\) means the genuine idempotent
\[
\omega_2\in\widehat{\mathbf Z},
\]
with \(2\)-component \(1\) and every odd-primary component \(0\); the integer is only its required residue modulo \(85667662080\). These are exactly the submission and verifier conventions. 

Turn 102 verified the two interfaces most capable of reversing a global count: the pure-determinant Shapiro calculation and the central-cover exact-image transform.  The proof can now be compressed into the following single theorem.

### Fixed-frame exact-image theorem

Let \(\Gamma_A\) denote the profinite marked object defined by the candidate, and let
\[
W_A=\langle\!\langle x_0,x_1\rangle\!\rangle .
\]
On the local side put \(\Gamma_F=G_{\mathbf Q_2}\) and let \(W_F\) be wild inertia.

Let \(G\) be finite, \(L\triangleleft G\) a normal \(2\)-subgroup, and
\[
H=G/L.
\]
Fix:

\[
\alpha:T_{\mathrm{tame}}\twoheadrightarrow H,
\qquad
T_{\mathrm{tame}}=\langle\sigma,\tau\mid\tau^\sigma=\tau^2\rangle,
\]
an elementary abelian scalar decoration \(\theta:G\to E\), and a prescribed scalar-character tuple \(\psi\).

For every \(J\le G\) satisfying \(JL=G\), let \(\mathcal E_\Gamma(J)\) be the maps \(\varphi:\Gamma\to G\) such that

\[
\operatorname{im}\varphi=J,\qquad
\overline\varphi=\alpha,\qquad
\varphi(W_\Gamma)\le J\cap L,\qquad
\theta\varphi=\psi .
\]

Then
\[
\boxed{\;
|\mathcal E_{\Gamma_A}(J)|
=
|\mathcal E_{\Gamma_F}(J)|
\;}
\tag{103.1}
\]
for every such decorated marked target, tame frame, and exact image \(J\).

The proof is a strong induction on \(|J\cap L|\). Replacing \(G\) by \(J\), it suffices to treat \(J=G\).

### 1. The terminal scalar branch

Suppose every \(G\)-chief factor in \(L\) is trivial. Let \(H_2\) be the maximal \(2\)-quotient of \(H\). Then the odd kernel
\[
N=\ker(H\to H_2)
\]
has a unique normal lift to \(G\), centralizes \(L\), and
\[
G\cong H\times_{H_2}Q
\tag{103.2}
\]
for a finite \(2\)-group \(Q\).

Moreover, projection to \(Q\) gives an exact image-poset equivalence
\[
\left\{J\le G:\operatorname{pr}_H(J)=H\right\}
\longleftrightarrow
\left\{Q'\le Q:Q'\twoheadrightarrow H_2\right\},
\qquad
J=H\times_{H_2}Q'.
\tag{103.3}
\]

Both sources have the same marked maximal pro-\(2\) quotient
\[
\Pi=
\left\langle
\sigma,x_0,x_1
\ \middle|\
x_0^2x_1^3x_1^\sigma=1
\right\rangle_{\mathrm{pro}\text{-}2}.
\tag{103.4}
\]
Thus every fixed-frame decorated exact-image stratum in (103.3) is literally the same compatible epimorphism set from \(\Pi\). This closes the entire scalar tower at once, including all proper images.

### 2. Collapse of the first active block

Assume now that \(L\) has a nontrivial tame chief factor. Choose
\[
S\triangleleft P\triangleleft G,
\qquad
P/S\simeq V,
\]
where all chief factors below \(S\) are scalar and \(V\) is the first nontrivial simple \(\mathbf F_2[H]\)-module.

Choose \(K\triangleleft G\), \(K\le P\), minimal subject to \(KS=P\), and put
\[
R=\Phi(K).
\]

Let \(N\triangleleft H\) be the odd kernel of the maximal \(2\)-quotient, and choose an odd Hall lift \(\widetilde N\). Since the chief factors of \(S\) are trivial, coprime action gives
\[
[S,\widetilde N]=1.
\tag{103.5}
\]
Also \(R\le K\cap S\), so \(\widetilde N\) centralizes \(R\).

Set \(C=C_K(R)\). It is characteristic in \(K\), hence normal in \(G\). The three-subgroup lemma gives
\[
[K,\widetilde N]\le C.
\]
The action of \(N\) on \(V\) is nontrivial; otherwise it would factor through a \(2\)-group, whose only simple module in characteristic \(2\) is trivial. Hence
\[
[V,N]=V,
\qquad
[K,\widetilde N]S=P.
\]
Thus \(CS=P\), and minimality of \(K\) forces \(C=K\). Therefore
\[
R\le Z(K).
\tag{103.6}
\]

Since \(K/R\) is elementary abelian, squares lie centrally in \(R\), and commutators have order at most \(2\). Consequently
\[
f:K\to R,\qquad f(k)=k^4
\]
is a \(G\)-equivariant homomorphism. It vanishes on \([K,\widetilde N]\), hence on its \(G\)-normal closure, which again equals \(K\) by minimality. Therefore
\[
K^4=1,\qquad
R=\Phi(K)=K^2[K,K]\ \text{is central elementary abelian}.
\tag{103.7}
\]

This direct collapse removes every finite-group classification or critical-subgroup input. 

Put
\[
M=K/R,\qquad
T_0=(K\cap S)/R.
\]
Minimality gives
\[
T_0=\operatorname{rad}_{\mathbf F_2[G/K]}(M),
\qquad
M/T_0\simeq V,
\qquad
(M^\vee)^{G/K}=0.
\tag{103.8}
\]

### 3. The elementary \(M\)-stage

For a generated lower tuple \(\rho\) and any elementary \(G/K\)-module \(A\), use the finite relation-deformation complex
\[
C_\rho^\bullet(A):
0\longrightarrow A
\longrightarrow A^4
\longrightarrow A^2
\longrightarrow0,
\tag{103.9}
\]
whose final map is the linearization of the tame and wild relation defects.

Reducing the \(\omega_2\)-words in finite Heisenberg extensions makes all first and mixed second derivatives ordinary finite-word calculations. Common-word naturality and dévissage give perfect duality
\[
H_\rho^i(A)\times H_\rho^{2-i}(A^\vee)
\longrightarrow\mathbf F_2.
\tag{103.10}
\]
In particular,
\[
\operatorname{Ob}_\rho(A)^\vee\simeq(A^\vee)^{G/K},
\qquad
\dim Z_\rho^1(A)
=
2\dim A+\dim(A^\vee)^{G/K}.
\tag{103.11}
\]

For \(A=M\), (103.8) yields
\[
\operatorname{Ob}_\rho(M)=0,
\qquad
|Z_\rho^1(M)|=2^{2\dim M}.
\tag{103.12}
\]
The candidate and local source therefore have the same unrestricted \(M\)-lift multiplicity. Every proper exact image has strictly smaller marked wild kernel, so the induction hypothesis converts unrestricted equality into exact-image equality for \(G/R\).

### 4. The central \(R\)-stage

Set
\[
B=G/R,\qquad C=G/K,\qquad
D=(R^\vee)^B.
\]
Finite-Heisenberg duality identifies \(D\) with the full character group of the \(R\)-valued relation-obstruction space.

For each \(0\ne\lambda\in D\), push the extension out along \(\lambda:R\to\mathbf F_2\). The resulting quadratic map on \(M\) satisfies
\[
q_\lambda|_{T_0}=0,
\qquad
b_\lambda(T_0,M)=0,
\tag{103.13}
\]
and descends to a nonzero nonsingular invariant quadratic form
\[
\bar q_\lambda:V\to\mathbf F_2.
\tag{103.14}
\]

Thus the scalar pushout has only two possibilities.

If its filtration-one edge is nonzero, the obstruction is a nonzero affine functional under the perfect deformation pairing, so exactly half of the unrestricted lift torsor survives.

If the edge is zero, transgression forces the exposed \(V\)-extension to split. A marking- and decoration-preserving shear removes every remaining mixed edge, leaving a pure determinant form plus an inflated scalar constant.

For the candidate, with tame actions \(S,T\) and
\[
U=S^{\omega_2},
\]
the determinant form is
\[
Q_A(c)=
\begin{cases}
q(c),&T=1,\\[2mm]
q(c)+B(c,U^{-1}c),&V^T=0.
\end{cases}
\tag{103.15}
\]
The Wall calculation gives
\[
\#Q_A^{-1}(0)=
\begin{cases}
2^{d-1}-2^{d/2-1},&V\text{ unramified},\\
2^{d-1}+2^{d/2-1},&V\text{ ramified}.
\end{cases}
\tag{103.16}
\]

The local determinant form has exactly the same zero counts. In the ramified case, faithful-image projectivity permits an \(H\)-split embedding into a free module. After first separating the pure determinant class, each quadratic orbit monomial becomes under Shapiro a corestriction of
\[
\alpha_r\smile g\alpha_s
\quad\text{or}\quad
\alpha_r^2.
\]
For deep-unit classes \(\alpha_r\in U_{e+1}\),
\[
U_{e+1}^{\perp}=U_e,\qquad
U_{e+1}\subseteq U_e,\qquad
-1\in U_e,
\]
so all cross terms vanish and
\[
\alpha_r^2=\alpha_r\smile(-1)=0
\]
before corestriction. The deep-unit subspace is half-dimensional and totally singular, hence the ramified form is hyperbolic. The unramified form is uniquely the norm–trace minus form. 

### 5. One transform controls generation and inflated constants

For a marked central cover
\[
p:\widetilde Y\twoheadrightarrow Y,
\qquad
\ker p\simeq C_2,
\]
and an exact-image subgroup \(J\le Y\), let \(u_\Gamma(p,J)\) count exact-image maps to \(J\) whose pullback obstruction vanishes. Every such map has exactly
\[
|\operatorname{Hom}(\Gamma,C_2)|=8
\]
lifts. Partitioning those lifts by exact image gives
\[
8u_\Gamma(p,J)
=
\sum_{\substack{\widetilde J\le p^{-1}(J)\\p(\widetilde J)=J}}
|\mathcal E_\Gamma(\widetilde J)|.
\tag{103.17}
\]

This identity is used twice and only twice.

For a proper weighted stratum \(J<B=G/R\),
\[
|\widetilde J\cap L|
\le
2|J\cap(L/R)|
\le
|L/R|
<
|L|.
\tag{103.18}
\]

For the inflated determinant constant over \(C=G/K\),
\[
|\widetilde J\cap L|
\le
2|L/K|
<
|L|,
\tag{103.19}
\]
because the nontrivial simple head has dimension at least \(2\), so \(|K|\ge4\).

Thus every exact-image term on the right of (103.17) lies strictly below the current induction parameter. There is no circular central-cover target.

Finally, Fourier inversion gives
\[
\#\{\rho:o_\Gamma(\rho)=0\}
=
\frac1{|D|}
\sum_{\lambda\in D}
\left(2m_{\Gamma,\lambda}-|X_\Gamma|\right).
\tag{103.20}
\]
The \(\lambda=0\) term is the already-matched \(B\)-count. For \(\lambda\ne0\), the affine or determinant zero fibre agrees by (103.10) and (103.16), while (103.17) controls both proper-image subtraction and the inflated constant. Hence every Fourier coefficient agrees.

Once the \(R\)-obstruction vanishes, both sources have the same \(R\)-lift multiplicity. Every lift is surjective: if its image \(J\) maps onto \(G/R\), then
\[
(J\cap K)R=K.
\]
Because \(R=\Phi(K)\), this forces \(J\cap K=K\), and hence \(J=G\).

This completes the strong induction and proves (103.1).

### Global consequence

Summing (103.1) over all tame frames, taking trivial decoration, and setting
\[
L=O_2(G)
\]
gives
\[
|\operatorname{Sur}(\Gamma_A,G)|
=
|\operatorname{Sur}(G_{\mathbf Q_2},G)|
\tag{103.21}
\]
for every finite group \(G\).

Epimorphisms from the cofiltered marked object \(\Gamma_A\) are exactly the generating verifier tuples. Consequently the normalized counts also agree:
\[
\frac{|\operatorname{Sur}(\Gamma_A,G)|}{|\operatorname{Aut}(G)|}
=
\frac{|\operatorname{Sur}(G_{\mathbf Q_2},G)|}{|\operatorname{Aut}(G)|}.
\tag{103.22}
\]
The fixed-frame construction and exact-image induction were introduced precisely to avoid aggregate tame-map and generation-distribution errors. 

Equality of all finite surjection counts, together with finite generation of \(\Gamma_A\), then gives
\[
\Gamma_A\simeq G_{\mathbf Q_2}
\]
by the one-sided profinite reconstruction lemma.

**ESTABLISHED**: Consolidated Turns 74–102 into a single strong induction on exact-image wild-kernel size. The direct active-block collapse, finite-Heisenberg deformation duality, simple-head determinant reduction, pure-determinant Shapiro calculation, and central-cover transform now occupy explicit, nonoverlapping roles. Both applications of the central-cover transform have strict descent, so the proof contains no same-size circular target.

**GAP**: The private 5402-group verifier remains unrun. The proof is now structurally consolidated, but it still needs independent line-by-line manuscript review and formal typesetting.

**NEXT**: Convert the theorem above into the standalone submission manuscript, append the unchanged verifier block, and send that exact block to the official verifier.
